title: 7.1 and 7.2 practice
description: Practice what you learned in lessons 7.1 and 7.2.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the result of some bacteria evolving to release oxygen into the atmosphere billions of years ago?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It poisoned many anaerobic organisms and made the atmosphere suitable for oxygen-using life.
It caused Earth to lose its entire atmosphere.
It instantly cooled the entire planet.
It created the first human civilizations.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why is the current climate change event different from past climate changes?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It is caused by human activities, mainly the burning of fossil fuels.
It is happening during the Ice Age.
It is the first time the climate has ever changed.
It is making Earth’s atmosphere disappear.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did a warming climate impact human society between 5,000 and 9,000 years ago?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It allowed farming to spread and new complex societies to develop.
It forced humans to return to hunting and gathering.
It caused the oceans to freeze over.
It led to the extinction of all large animals.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How have past climate changes shaped human societies?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Shifts in climate have caused famines, migrations, and even political revolutions
They always led to peaceful cooperation between nations.
They only affected people living near the poles.
